angles.argument: California has frequent blackouts and also leads in EV adoption
search_queries: most EVs per capita US states

angles.argument: EV charging requires massive electricity, and grids are already strained by renewables intermittency
Texas 2021 freeze caused grid failure, and now they're pushing EVs which would make it worse
Rare earth mining for EV batteries devastates environments, just like coal did, so EVs are as dirty as fossil fuels for the grid
Europe's energy crisis led to power shortages, and high EV pushes there show the same pattern
One EV fire from battery overload shows how charging spikes overload local grids
Australia had grid instability from high renewables, now adding EVs will collapse it entirely
